lesson.menu_desc: Describing how a study was done with the passive voice: the sample was analyzed, the data were obtained -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Explaining Methods & Processes
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patricia Ruffo | Aula 13 -- Explaining Methods & Processes

listenings.text: Good morning. Let me explain how this six-month study was conducted, step by step. First, the protocol was approved by the ethics committee before any work began. Then, two hundred participants were recruited and divided into two groups. Each participant was measured at the start, and the same procedure was repeated every month. The blood samples were collected by trained nurses and stored at a controlled temperature. After collection, the samples were analyzed in the central laboratory, and the results were obtained within forty-eight hours. To make the process reliable, every measurement was standardized, so the same method was used for every patient. Finally, the complete dataset was reviewed by an independent statistician before the figures were published. I want to highlight one point: because the procedure was standardized and the protocol was followed carefully, the results can be trusted and the study could be repeated by another team.

Hi Patricia, this is David from the lab. I just finished reading your methods section, and it is very clear. I like how you explain that the samples were collected by trained staff and that every measurement was standardized. Two small comments before we submit. First, please mention exactly how the dataset was analyzed, because reviewers always ask which software was used. Second, in the sentence where you say the protocol was approved, add the date, so it is completely transparent. Everything else is solid. The process is well described, the steps are in the right order, and the passive voice keeps the focus on the method, not on the people. Let us go through it together tomorrow morning and then we can finalize the manuscript. Thanks again, and great work on the write-up.

extra_audio.text: First, say that the protocol was approved. Next, explain how the participants were recruited. After that, describe how the samples were collected and measured. Then, mention how the dataset was analyzed. Finally, state that the results were obtained and reviewed.
